title: Create submission tracker for query rounds, agent replies, and revision follow-ups
description:
## Problem After manuscript completion, writers lose context across submissions (who was queried, when responses arrived, what changes were requested). Spreadsheets and inbox threads break continuity and reduce follow-through. ## Target user Writers preparing to query agents, submit to contests, or run structured beta-reader rounds. ## Outcome / KPI - 35% of users who export a submission package also create at least one tracked submission entry. - 20% increase in completed follow-up actions within target response windows. - Reduction in missed response deadlines and duplicate submissions. ## Scope - Add submission tracker workspace tied to a manuscript. - Track entries with status, recipient, sent date, response date, response type, and next action date. - Provide timeline and board views (e.g., Drafting, Submitted, Requested Revisions, Closed). - Add reminder nudges for overdue follow-ups and pending requested revisions. - Link each submission entry to exported package version or manuscript snapshot used at send time. ## Non-goals - Automated email sending from the app. - Marketplace/discovery functionality for publishers or agents. - Contract/legal workflow management. ## Acceptance criteria - User can create, edit, and archive submission entries per manuscript. - Board/timeline views accurately reflect submission status transitions. - Reminder state appears for overdue follow-up dates. - Entry can reference a specific export/snapshot artifact so writer knows exactly what was sent. - Data persists and is included in manuscript-level backups. ## Priority rationale This extends the product from drafting into real publishing workflow outcomes, reducing drop-off after completion. It complements the existing package builder while creating a practical end-to-end moat for serious writers. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research --- Recovered from BIN-89 after checkout 409. [CEO]: Assigned to Market Research. [Market Research]: Checkout release requested: 409 [Market Research]: ## Decision approve ## Differentiation thesis This should be approved because it extends the product from "write a manuscript" into "ship the manuscript, track outcomes, and close the loop" inside one workspace. That is a meaningful differentiation angle versus drafting-first tools and community-first platforms. ## Competitor evidence - LivingWriter’s core positioning and docs emphasize outlining, chapter/scene organization, boards, and drafting workflow (not a post-draft query/submission operations layer): https://livingwriter.com/en and https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/boards/planning-board/standard-grid - Campfire emphasizes manuscript + worldbuilding modules (characters, locations, timeline, maps, etc.), again focused on writing/world context rather than submission pipeline tracking: https://campfirewriting.com/apps and https://www.campfirewriting.com/pricing - Inkitt’s writer flow is centered on reader feedback/community and publication through engagement, not agent query CRM-style tracking tied to manuscript package versions: https://www.inkitt.com/writers - QueryTracker exists as a separate tool specifically for tracking query statuses, replies, and submission state; this validates real demand but also shows writers currently split workflow across products: https://querytracker.net/help/tracking and https://querytracker.net/help/status/ ## Risks / trade-offs - Risk of becoming a generic Kanban/CRM if manuscript linkage is weak. - Additional complexity in reminders/status taxonomies can increase cognitive load for writers who only draft. - If data model is too query-agent centric, it may not transfer cleanly to contests/beta-reader workflows. ## Recommendation Proceed, with differentiation guardrails: ensure each submission entry is manuscript-native (snapshot/package version attached, revision request loop integrated, and follow-up intelligence focused on writer outcomes). Keep UX opinionated and writing-first, not a generic pipeline tool. ## Handoff Assign to: Design [CEO]: Assigned to Design. [CEO]: Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout. [Design]: ## UX goal Give writers a manuscript-native submission command center that makes follow-ups impossible to miss: capture each submission in under 30 seconds, see pipeline health at a glance, and act on overdue/revision tasks in one click. ## Information hierarchy 1. Primary context: manuscript identity + quick-add submission action. 2. Operational urgency: overdue follow-ups and pending revision requests (always visible near top). 3. Workflow views: Board (status flow) and Timeline (date flow) as equal first-class modes. 4. Entry detail: recipient, response, next action, and linked package/snapshot artifact. 5. Historical/archive: closed/archived entries visually de-emphasized but searchable. ## Layout and components - Place Submission Tracker inside the existing story workspace (`/story/[id]`) as a third mode alongside Editor and Corkboard for one-click mode switching. - Header row mirrors current story page pattern: story title input + mode switch + primary CTA (`Add Submission`). - Add a compact KPI strip directly below header: `Active`, `Awaiting Response`, `Overdue`, `Revisions Requested`. - Main canvas supports two tabs/views: - Board view: columns `Drafting`, `Submitted`, `Requested Revisions`, `Closed`; card-based entries with top-line recipient + date badges + next action chip. - Timeline view: chronological grouped rows (by week/month), emphasizing `Sent`, `Response`, `Next action` milestones. - Right-side or slide-over detail panel for create/edit so users do not lose context of board/timeline position. - Entry card fields (visible): recipient name, channel/type, sent date, response type/date, next action date, linked package/snapshot id. ## Interaction states - Idle: board/timeline populated; overdue chips use high-contrast neutral emphasis (no bright warning colors). - Loading: skeleton cards/rows using existing pulse pattern from corkboard. - Empty: guided first-run state with one CTA (`Add first submission`) and short 2-step helper text. - Error: inline panel/banner at top of canvas with retry action, preserving unsaved form state when possible. - Success: non-blocking confirmation toast/inline confirmation (`Submission saved`) and immediate card/timeline update in-place. ## Accessibility - Keyboard: full tab flow through mode switch, filters, cards, and detail form; Enter/Space opens card detail; Esc closes panel. - Focus: visible 2px focus rings consistent with current token usage (`--color-carbon-600`). - Contrast: use grayscale tokens with at least AA text contrast for status chips and metadata text. - Text clarity: plain-language labels (`Next action`, `Waiting on response`) instead of publishing jargon-only terms. ## Visual style - Follow current restrained grayscale system from `src/styles/tokens.css`; avoid introducing new accent palette unless product leadership approves. - Surfaces: white content on polar/fog containers for hierarchy; fashion-300 borders for structure; black/carbon for headings and active controls. - Typography: keep existing sans stack for UI chrome; maintain editor-like calm with generous spacing and low visual noise (Living Writer clarity bar). - Status styling should rely on weight, border, background tint steps, and iconography/labels instead of saturated colors. ## Responsive behavior - Desktop: board columns visible side-by-side with horizontal scroll if needed; detail panel as right drawer. - Tablet: reduce visible metadata per card; keep mode/filters sticky at top of tracker canvas. - Mobile: switch board to stacked status sections; timeline becomes condensed list; `Add Submission` remains persistent in header area. - Keep one-tap mode changes between Editor/Corkboard/Submissions at all breakpoints. ## Codebase alignment - Reviewed and aligned with existing shell/layout primitives: `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/components/AppShell/*`, `src/components/Header/*`, `src/components/ContentContainer/*`. - Existing story workspace composition in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` already supports mode-driven rendering; extend this pattern for Submission Tracker mode. - Reuse interaction language from `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/*` and card/grid behavior cues from `src/components/Corkboard/*` + `src/components/SceneCard/*`. - Keep styles tokenized per `src/styles/tokens.css` and CSS module approach used across `src/app/**/*.module.css`. ## Reuse vs new build - Reuse: - `ViewModeSwitch` interaction model (expand options). - Corkboard skeleton/toolbar/card spacing patterns. - AppShell/Header/ContentContainer frame and responsive conventions. - New build: - Submission entry card component(s). - Timeline row component(s). - Submission detail form drawer/panel. - Reminder/urgency chip component tuned to grayscale-only styling. ## Feasibility notes - Current story data is local and mock-driven (`src/lib/scenes.ts` pattern), so submission tracker UI should be designed to tolerate optimistic local state first, then API-backed persistence later without redesign. - Board and timeline both require a normalized status/date model; ensure statuses are finite and mapped consistently across views. - Linked artifact requirement implies an ID/reference field in UI even before full export artifact browsing exists. - Drag/drop in board can mirror current dnd-kit usage but should remain optional for v1 if data integrity risks are high. ## Constraints / implementation notes - Anchor new tracker mode in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` to preserve one-workspace flow. - Extend `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx` to include `submissions` state and accessible labeling. - Add tracker-specific modules under `src/components` with CSS modules, maintaining token-only color usage from `src/styles/tokens.css`. - Keep top-level page title behavior in `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx` coherent (consider `Story Workspace` naming if mode-specific title changes are needed). - Preserve existing mobile nav/header behavior in `AppShell` and avoid introducing layout shifts above 64px header. ## Handoff Assign to: Founding Engineer [CEO]: Assigned to Founding Engineer. [CEO]: Board: please release checkout for this issue (assignee got 409). See docs/PAPERCLIP_SETUP.md § Release a stuck checkout. ## Goal Add a Submission Tracker mode to the story workspace — a manuscript-native CRM for writers managing query rounds, agent replies, and revision follow-ups. Writers can create, edit, and archive submission entries, view them in Board or Timeline view, and get nudged on overdue follow-ups. ## Scope and files **New files:** - `src/types/submission.ts` — SubmissionEntry type, SubmissionStatus enum, and related types - `src/lib/submissions.ts` — localStorage CRUD (getSubmissions, addSubmission, updateSubmission, archiveSubmission) - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTracker.tsx` — top-level wrapper with KPI strip and board/timeline tab toggle - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTracker.module.css` - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionBoard.tsx` — board view with four status columns (Drafting → Submitted → Requested Revisions → Closed) - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionBoard.module.css` - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTimeline.tsx` — chronological list grouped by week/month showing Sent, Response, Next Action milestones - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTimeline.module.css` - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionCard.tsx` — shared card used in board and timeline views - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionCard.module.css` - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionPanel.tsx` — slide-over create/edit detail form panel - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionPanel.module.css` - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/OverdueChip.tsx` — inline urgency chip (grayscale-only, weight/border emphasis) - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/OverdueChip.module.css` - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/KpiStrip.tsx` — compact strip: Active, Awaiting Response, Overdue, Revisions Requested counts - `src/components/SubmissionTracker/KpiStrip.module.css` **Modified files:** - `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx` — add `'submissions'` to `ViewMode` union; add third "Submissions" button - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add submissions state + handler; render `` when mode === `'submissions'` - `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — add `.submissionsLayout` class mirroring `.corkboardLayout` pattern ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 1 — Define types (`src/types/submission.ts`) ```ts export type SubmissionStatus = 'drafting' | 'submitted' | 'requested_revisions' | 'closed'; export type ResponseType = 'full_request' | 'partial_request' | 'rejection' | 'no_response' | 'offer' | 'other'; export interface SubmissionEntry { id: string; // crypto.randomUUID() storyId: string; recipientName: string; // agent/contest/reader name channelType: string; // e.g. "Agent Query", "Contest", "Beta Reader" status: SubmissionStatus; sentDate: string | null; // ISO date string responseDate: string | null; responseType: ResponseType | null; nextActionDate: string | null; // when to follow up notes: string; linkedArtifactId: string | null; // reference to export/snapshot ID archivedAt: string | null; // null = active createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; } ``` ### Step 2 — Build localStorage CRUD lib (`src/lib/submissions.ts`) - `getSubmissions(storyId): SubmissionEntry[]` — reads `story-{storyId}-submissions` from localStorage; seeds empty array on first call - `addSubmission(storyId, fields): SubmissionEntry` — creates entry with `crypto.randomUUID()` id, timestamps; persists - `updateSubmission(storyId, id, partial): void` — merges changes, updates `updatedAt`; persists - `archiveSubmission(storyId, id): void` — sets `archivedAt` to ISO now; persists - `deleteSubmission(storyId, id): void` — hard delete (for edge cases only) - Follow the `getScenes` / `updateSceneStatus` pattern exactly (SSR guard, error handling, localStorage JSON) ### Step 3 — Add `'submissions'` to ViewModeSwitch In `ViewModeSwitch.tsx`: - Change `export type ViewMode = 'editor' | 'corkboard'` → `'editor' | 'corkboard' | 'submissions'` - Add third button after Corkboard: label `"Submissions"`, `aria-label="Switch to submissions view"`, `aria-pressed={mode === 'submissions'}` - No CSS changes needed — existing `.active`/`.inactive` classes apply ### Step 4 — Build OverdueChip (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/OverdueChip.tsx`) - Props: `nextActionDate: string | null` - Logic: if `nextActionDate` is in the past, render chip with label "Overdue" - Style: use border + font-weight emphasis only (no color outside grayscale tokens); small pill shape with `--border-radius-lg`, `--font-size-xs`, `--color-carbon-600` border, `--color-polar-100` background ### Step 5 — Build KpiStrip (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/KpiStrip.tsx`) - Props: `entries: SubmissionEntry[]` - Computes four counts from active (non-archived) entries: - Active: all non-archived, non-closed - Awaiting Response: status `submitted` with no `responseDate` - Overdue: `nextActionDate` is past - Revisions Requested: status `requested_revisions` - Renders as a compact flex row, each item a `
label
count
` pair - Separated by `--color-fashion-300` dividers - CSS module using tokens only ### Step 6 — Build SubmissionCard (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionCard.tsx`) - Props: `entry: SubmissionEntry; onClick: () => void` - Shows: recipient name (bold), channel/type, sent date, response type/date, next action date, `` if applicable, linked artifact ID (greyed if null) - Click opens the detail panel - Keyboard: `onKeyDown` handles Enter/Space → `onClick` - Role: `role="button"` with `tabIndex={0}` - CSS: white background, `--color-fashion-300` border, `--border-radius-md` corners, hover → `--color-polar-100` bg ### Step 7 — Build SubmissionBoard (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionBoard.tsx`) - Props: `entries: SubmissionEntry[]; onCardClick: (id: string) => void` - Four columns: `drafting`, `submitted`, `requested_revisions`, `closed` - Column headers: human-readable labels ("Drafting", "Submitted", "Requested Revisions", "Closed") - Filters active entries by status; archived entries never shown in board - No drag-drop for v1 (per Design feasibility note: "optional for v1 if data integrity risks are high") - Empty column: shows a `

No entries

` placeholder - CSS: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr)` with gap and overflow-x auto for tablet ### Step 8 — Build SubmissionTimeline (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTimeline.tsx`) - Props: `entries: SubmissionEntry[]; onCardClick: (id: string) => void` - Group active entries by sent week (using `sentDate`, fall back to `createdAt` for drafts) - Sort groups descending (newest first) - Each group: header `

` with week label (e.g. "Week of Mar 3, 2025"), then list of `` rows - Entries without sent date appear under "Not yet sent" ### Step 9 — Build SubmissionPanel (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionPanel.tsx`) - Props: `storyId: string; entry: SubmissionEntry | null; onSave: (entry: SubmissionEntry) => void; onClose: () => void; onArchive?: (id: string) => void` - When `entry` is null → create mode ("Add Submission"); when non-null → edit mode - Form fields: recipientName (text, required), channelType (text), status (select), sentDate (date), responseDate (date), responseType (select), nextActionDate (date), linkedArtifactId (text, optional label "Package/snapshot ID"), notes (textarea) - Save: calls `onSave` with merged entry; parent handles persistence - Archive: calls `onArchive(entry.id)` then `onClose`; only shown in edit mode - Renders as a right-side drawer/panel overlay using fixed positioning; backdrop click triggers close - Esc key listener calls `onClose` - Success state: parent shows inline confirmation near card (handled in SubmissionTracker); no toast lib needed — add a `savedId` state in SubmissionTracker that briefly highlights the saved card - Accessibility: focus trap within panel; first field auto-focused on open; close button with `aria-label="Close submission panel"` ### Step 10 — Build SubmissionTracker (`src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTracker.tsx`) - Props: `storyId: string` - State: `entries`, `viewTab: 'board' | 'timeline'`, `panelEntry: SubmissionEntry | null`, `panelOpen: boolean`, `savedId: string | null` - On mount: `useEffect` loads entries from `getSubmissions(storyId)` - Handlers: - `handleAddClick` → sets `panelEntry = null`, `panelOpen = true` - `handleCardClick(id)` → finds entry, sets `panelEntry`, `panelOpen = true` - `handleSave(entry)` → calls add or update lib function, refreshes entries, sets `savedId = entry.id`, clears after 2s - `handleArchive(id)` → calls `archiveSubmission`, refreshes entries, closes panel - `handleClose` → sets `panelOpen = false` - Layout: header row (story title input + ViewModeSwitch + "Add Submission" button), KpiStrip, view tab toggle (Board / Timeline), canvas area - Empty state: when entries length === 0, show centered "No submissions yet" with "Add first submission" CTA button and 2-step helper text - Renders `` conditionally when `panelOpen` ### Step 11 — Integrate into story page (`src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`) - Import `SubmissionTracker` from `@/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTracker` - In the JSX return, add a third branch: `else if (viewMode === 'submissions')` renders: ```tsx
``` - Note: ViewModeSwitch is rendered inside SubmissionTracker (along with the title input and Add button), so the page just wraps with the layout div ### Step 12 — Add `.submissionsLayout` to page.module.css ```css .submissionsLayout { height: 100%; background-color: var(--color-white); overflow: hidden; display: flex; flex-direction: column; } ``` ### Step 13 — Manual smoke test (See Test plan below) ## Test plan **Unit / component checks:** - `getSubmissions` returns `[]` for new storyId; after `addSubmission`, returns 1 entry sorted by createdAt - `archiveSubmission` sets `archivedAt`, entry excluded from board/timeline views - `OverdueChip` renders when `nextActionDate` is yesterday, hidden when null or future - `KpiStrip` correctly counts Overdue and Revisions Requested from mock entries - `SubmissionPanel` in create mode: Save with empty recipientName shows required field state; Save with valid data calls `onSave` **Manual checks (in-browser):** 1. Open any story; confirm "Submissions" appears as third mode in ViewModeSwitch 2. Switch to Submissions mode; confirm empty state renders with CTA 3. Click "Add first submission"; confirm panel slides in from right, Esc closes it 4. Fill in recipientName + status + sentDate; Save; confirm card appears in board under correct column, KPI counts update, card briefly highlights 5. Click card; confirm panel opens in edit mode with pre-filled values 6. Edit recipientName; Save; confirm board card updates immediately 7. Archive entry; confirm card disappears from board, KPI decrements 8. Set nextActionDate to yesterday; confirm OverdueChip appears on card and Overdue KPI increments 9. Switch to Timeline view; confirm entry grouped by sent week 10. Resize browser to mobile width; confirm board becomes stacked sections, panel still usable **Keyboard/a11y checks:** - Tab through mode switch → "Add Submission" button → KPI strip → board columns → cards; all focusable with visible ring - Enter on card opens panel; Esc closes; focus returns to card **Commands (if tests exist):** ```bash npm run typecheck && npm run lint ``` ## Risks and mitigations - **ViewMode union change breaks existing TS callers**: Only `page.tsx` uses `ViewMode` directly — checked; adding `'submissions'` is additive. - **Panel focus trap complexity**: Keep it simple — auto-focus first field on open, listen for Esc. Full focus-trap library not needed for v1. - **Overdue date comparison timezone issues**: Compare dates as UTC ISO strings truncated to date part (`new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)`) to avoid timezone edge cases. - **SubmissionTracker re-renders**: `entries` state lives in SubmissionTracker, refreshed only after mutations — no unnecessary renders. ## Rollback strategy All new code is additive. Reverting means: remove the `'submissions'` branch in `page.tsx`, remove it from `ViewModeSwitch`, and delete the new component/lib/type files. localStorage key (`story-{id}-submissions`) remains but is harmless. ## Observability - Follow existing `console.error` pattern in lib functions on localStorage parse failure (mirrors `getScenes`) - No new analytics instrumentation in v1 ## Acceptance mapping | Criterion | Coverage | |---|---| | User can create, edit, and archive submission entries per manuscript | Steps 2, 9, 10 — add/update/archive via panel | | Board/timeline views accurately reflect submission status transitions | Steps 7, 8 — board columns + timeline grouping | | Reminder state appears for overdue follow-up dates | Steps 4, 5 — OverdueChip + KpiStrip Overdue count | | Entry can reference a specific export/snapshot artifact | Step 1 — `linkedArtifactId` field; Step 9 — panel input | | Data persists and is included in manuscript-level backups | Step 2 — localStorage under `story-{id}-submissions` key; follows same backup pattern as scenes | ## Handoff Assign to: Code Monkey
